topStories.description: Energy prices are surging as the Iran war disrupts supply, raising risks for the US, China and Europe.

topStories.title: Mojtaba Khamenei's ‘god’s will' escape during US-Israel strike that killed his father: What does leaked audio reveal?
topStories.description: Leaked audio reportedly reveals that Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader, narrowly escaped a US-Israeli missile strike on February 28 by stepping outside moments before the attack, while his wife and son tragically died in the explosion.

topStories.title: Gecko Robotics lands the largest U.S. Navy robotics deal yet
topStories.description: Gecko Robotics inked a five-year deal to help the U.S. Navy monitor and predict needed maintenance on its fleet of ships.

topStories.title: Adjournment motion over MNREGA disallowed, Cong walks out of Chhattisgarh House
topStories.description: Leader of Opposition Charan Das Mahant said the Congress had indicated its plan to move an adjournment motion and then boycott proceedings

topStories.title: RRS chief urges Karnataka not to increase Almatti dam height
topStories.description: RRS president Dr. Kuncham Venkatasubba Reddy said he had written a letter to PM Narendra Modi and also apprised Karnataka State water resources department Minister D.K. Shiva Kumar about the problems that the farmers of the three States face if the height of Almatti dam is increased

topStories.title: Hormuz Shutdown Affects Asia's Crude Oil Supply, Pipelines Can't Cover Loss
topStories.description: Around 20 million barrels of oil  a fifth of the world's seaborne crude ships through the Strait of Hormuz every day but that volume has been severely limited following US and Israel attacks on Iran,...

topStories.title: Supreme Court strikes down law limiting maternity leave to adoption of children under 3 months
topStories.description: The Supreme Court also asked the Centre to come out with a provision recognising paternity leave as a social security benefit

topStories.title: Nigeria’s first state visit since 1989: President Bola Tinubu heads to UK
topStories.description: British pomp and pageantry await the Nigerian leader, but some in the diaspora are sceptical about his trip.

topStories.title: Did Iran offer 440 kg of enriched uranium to US negotiators 48 hours before strikes? Araghchi reveals insider details
topStories.description: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said that giving up the uranium was one of the elements in the then-ongoing talks with the US. According to him, this showed the willingness of Iran to let go of the material.

topStories.title: Where is Iran’s 440 kg of nuclear stockpile, and who controls it? Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reveals
topStories.description: In a recent interview, Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi was quizzed about Iran's 440 kg stockpile of nuclear material as well as the US-Iran nuclear talks that were underway before Operation Epic Fury was launched. Here's what he revealed.

dailyNews.title: Iran is targeting ships in response to US attacks — can the US stop them?
dailyNews.description: As the US-Israeli war against Iran continues, we look at a scenario reminiscent of the tanker war of the 1980s

dailyNews.title: Who is Sofia Firdous, Odisha Congress MLA who allegedly picked BJP in Rajya Sabha polls?
dailyNews.description: The story began two days before the Rajya Sabha elections, when Sofia Firdous expressed her dissatisfaction with the Congress decision to support the BJD.

dailyNews.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 18 of US-Israel attacks?
dailyNews.description: The US-Israeli attacks target Iranian cities as the conflict spreads to the region and causes a global energy crisis.

dailyNews.title: Two-time NCAA champion Jalen Brunson reveals what it takes to be great in March Madness
dailyNews.description: Jalen Brunson shared valuable championship advice for March Madness hopefuls, drawing from his two NCAA titles with Villanova and current NBA experience.

dailyNews.title: Mark Normand says Late Night is 'dying' — how the stand-up comic keeps up in a changing comedy scene
dailyNews.description: Comedian Mark Normand explained how social media-fueled partisanship prevents audiences from enjoying comedy as people focus on determining political stances instead.

dailyNews.title: Specific amount of nightly sleep may lower diabetes risk, researchers find
dailyNews.description: A large observational study suggests that around seven hours of nightly sleep is associated with lower insulin resistance, while too little or too much sleep may worsen metabolic health.

dailyNews.title: Trump admin asks Spanberger, Virginia officials not release illegal charged with groping high school girls
dailyNews.description: The Department of Homeland Security has requested that Gov. Abigail Spanberger and officials in Fairfax County not release an illegal charged with groping several high school girls.

dailyNews.title: Trump voter ID push faces Senate test as GOP rebels threaten to sink bill
dailyNews.description: The Senate GOP can't beat the math for the talking filibuster, instead forcing Senate Democrats to challenge the bill. But not everyone in the GOP supports the SAVE America Act.

dailyNews.title: Why Gulf states aren’t joining the war against Iran — despite attacks on their soil
dailyNews.description: Gulf states maintain restraint despite reported Iranian attacks on Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar and Kuwait, prioritizing regional stability.

dailyNews.title: Fraud scrutiny clouds Schumer’s Obamacare credit push after his Pentagon ‘luxury’ spending jab
dailyNews.description: Schumer slams Pentagon's $93B spending spree, pushes ACA subsidies instead. Critics cite widespread fraud in healthcare program with phantom enrollees.

dailyNews.title: Mullin wins backing from China, Cuba hawks — and some Democrats — as critics question fitness to lead DHS
dailyNews.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin draws bipartisan support for DHS secretary role, with China hawks and Democrats backing Trump's nomination pick despite critics.

dailyNews.title: Temple from time of Christ reveals powerful society lost to history, archaeologists say
dailyNews.description: Archaeologists uncovered a 2,000-year-old temple in Denmark at Hedegård, revealing insights into a mysterious Iron Age power center with luxury goods and Roman connections.

topStories.title: MORNING GLORY: What will Donald Trump's legacy be as a wartime president?
topStories.description: Analysis compares Trump's tough stance on Iran to previous presidents, examining decades of U.S.-Iran conflict and nuclear weapons concerns in the region.

topStories.title: LIZ PEEK: Hollywood trashes Trump again — and proves just how out of touch it is
topStories.description: Hollywood actors make political speeches at 2026 Oscars, but viewership plummets from 55 million in 1998 to just 20 million today as audiences continue to reject elite messaging.

topStories.title: SEN WICKER: Ending China’s drone dominance with a made-in-America revival
topStories.description: Congress and Donald Trump allocated $2.5 billion for Pentagon small drone purchases, launching Drone Dominance program with American vendors to compete globally.

topStories.title: Why Trump is denouncing the media’s Iran war coverage as too negative – boosted by rhetorical FCC backing
topStories.description: Media tensions escalate as the Trump administration criticizes press coverage of the Iran conflict, with officials questioning journalistic approaches to war.

topStories.title: Admiral’s Comments Undercut Pentagon’s Cluster Munition Policy
topStories.description: The first Trump administration defended cluster munitions as “legitimate,” but on Monday, Adm. Brad Cooper condemned them as “inherently indiscriminate.”

topStories.title: What Are Trump’s Positions on Iran? They Can Change by the Sentence.
topStories.description: The president is no stranger to staking out contradictory stands, part of what his aides say is his negotiating style. But on Iran, his shifting positions are colliding with the consequences of war.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Allies Reject Trump’s Demands for Warships, and Bovino to Retire From Border Patrol
dailyNews.description: Plus, the champion of “Swedish death cleaning” dies at 91.

dailyNews.title: War Isn’t the Only Thing on Trump’s Mind
dailyNews.description: President Trump spoke to journalists for about 40 minutes before holding a news conference on Monday, offering a glimpse into his priorities.

dailyNews.title: Richard Grenell: From Trump Cabinet Hopeful to Ex-Kennedy Center Manager
dailyNews.description: Richard Grenell once hoped to be President Trump’s secretary of state. Instead, Mr. Trump just replaced him as Kennedy Center president.

dailyNews.title: Oil Begins Flowing Through California Pipeline Under Trump Order
dailyNews.description: Oil had not flowed through the pipeline since a 2015 rupture caused an environmental disaster on California’s Central Coast. It sets up a new fight between the Trump administration and state officials.

dailyNews.title: Trump Officials Weigh New Plan to Stop Offshore Wind Farms
dailyNews.description: Proposed settlements would block wind farms off New York State and North Carolina, according to documents reviewed by The New York Times.

dailyNews.title: Cuba Ready to Accept Outside Investment, Top Official Says
dailyNews.description: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
dailyNews.description: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.

dailyNews.title: Senate to Take Up Voter Bill Sought by Trump but Opposed by Democrats
dailyNews.description: The Republican leader said he couldn’t overcome a filibuster but, under relentless pressure from the president and the far right, would put Democrats on the record against the restrictive voter I.D. bill.

dailyNews.title: Israel targets more Iran leaders as allies reject Trump's calls for help
dailyNews.description: Israel says it killed Iran's top security official Ali Larijani, as America's European allies reject Trump's demands for help in the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Iran war keeps Strait of Hormuz paralyzed and gas prices on the rise
dailyNews.description: The Iran war could escalate further as President Trump threatens to hit key oil infrastructure if Tehran doesn't drop its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: 3/9: Face the Nation
dailyNews.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," after a whiplash week of on-again, off-again tariff announcements, Canadian ambassador to the U.S. Kirsten Hillman joins to discuss the costs and consequences of a trade war. Plus, former White House Russia expert Fiona Hill discusses President Trump's diplomatic pivot on Ukraine.

dailyNews.title: Alleged pipe bomber argues charges should be tossed under Jan. 6 pardons
dailyNews.description: A man who was accused of planting pipe bombs outside the RNC and DNC on the eve of the Jan. 6 attack is arguing he is covered by President Trump's sweeping pardons of alleged Jan. 6 rioters.
